perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 8.2, if the Company determines to solicit additional equity financing subsequent to exercise of the Option but prior to Commencement of Production (hereinafter defined) at the El Aguila project, it shall provide written notice to that effect to HOC and HOC shall be entitled to exclusively provide such financing upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. For purposes of the preceding sentence, "Commencement of Production" shall be defined as the production and delivery to the point of sale (refiner) by the Company (either directly or through a subsidiary) of not less than 4,000 ounces of gold within a 45 day period. HOC shall have ten (10) Business Days from delivery of such notice in which to notify the Company that it desires to provide all of such financing (the "Financing Election"). If HOC delivers the Financing Election, the purchase price for each share shall be equal to eighty percent (80%) of the average closing price of the Shares during the thirty (30) calendar days preceding the date HOC delivers the Financing Election. Closing of the subscription, purchase and sale shall be at such place and time as the Parties agree but not more than ten (10) days from delivery of the Financing Election. If HOC delivers the Financing Election, one or more of the HOC Entities shall pay the purchase price for, and the Company shall issue, additional Shares, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances. The Parties shall exchange representations and warranties, in form and in substance substantially similar to those provided in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Shares. In the event HOC fails to provide the Financing Election as set forth above, the Company shall be free to obtain such financing from one or more additional parties, free of any obligation to HOC.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 8.2, if the Company determines to solicit additional equity financing subsequent to exercise of the Option but prior to Operation Start (hereinafter defined) at the El Aguila project, it shall provide written notice to that effect to HOC and HOC shall be entitled to exclusively provide such financing upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. For purposes of the preceding sentence, "Operation Start" shall be defined as the extraction and transport to the processing plant by the Company (either directly or through a subsidiary) of not less than 4,000 ounces of gold within a 45 day period. HOC shall have ten (10) Business Days from delivery of such notice in which to notify the Company that it desires to provide all of such financing (the "Financing Election"). If HOC delivers the Financing Election, the purchase price for each share shall be equal to eighty percent (80%) of the average closing price of the Shares during the thirty (30) calendar days preceding the date HOC delivers the Financing Election. Closing of the subscription, purchase and sale shall be at such place and time as the Parties agree but not more than ten (10) days from delivery of the Financing Election. If HOC delivers the Financing Election, one or more of the HOC Entities shall pay the purchase price for, and the Company shall issue, additional Shares, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances. The Parties shall exchange representations and warranties, in form and in substance substantially similar to those provided in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Shares. In the event HOC fails to provide the Financing Election as set forth above, the Company shall be free to obtain such financing from one or more additional parties, free of any obligation to HOC.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by using the term "Operation Start" instead of "Commencement of Production" and redefining it in a conflicting way. The original definition specifies 'production and delivery to the point of sale (refiner)', implying a completed refinement process. The new definition is 'extraction and transport to the processing plant', which is an earlier stage in the process. This creates uncertainty about when HOC's exclusive financing rights end, since 'Operation Start' and 'Commencement of Production' are used inconsistently, and the specific stage of operation required to trigger the end of this right is now ambiguous. This contradicts with in the same section, where the text mentions: Subject to the provisions of Section 8.2, if the Company determines to solicit additional equity financing subsequent to exercise of the Option but prior to Commencement of Production (hereinafter defined) at the El Aguila project, it shall provide written notice to that effect to HOC and HOC shall be entitled to exclusively provide such financing upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 8.2, if the Company determines to solicit additional equity financing subsequent to exercise of the Option but prior to Operation Start (hereinafter defined) at the El Aguila project, it shall provide written notice to that effect to HOC and HOC shall be entitled to exclusively provide such financing upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. For purposes of the preceding sentence, "Operation Start" shall be defined as the extraction and transport to the processing plant by the Company (either directly or through a subsidiary) of not less than 4,000 ounces of gold within a 45 day period. HOC shall have ten (10) Business Days from delivery of such notice in which to notify the Company that it desires to provide all of such financing (the "Financing Election"). If HOC delivers the Financing Election, the purchase price for each share shall be equal to eighty percent (80%) of the average closing price of the Shares during the thirty (30) calendar days preceding the date HOC delivers the Financing Election. Closing of the subscription, purchase and sale shall be at such place and time as the Parties agree but not more than ten (10) days from delivery of the Financing Election. If HOC delivers the Financing Election, one or more of the HOC Entities shall pay the purchase price for, and the Company shall issue, additional Shares, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances. The Parties shall exchange representations and warranties, in form and in substance substantially similar to those provided in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Shares. In the event HOC fails to provide the Financing Election as set forth above, the Company shall be free to obtain such financing from one or more additional parties, free of any obligation to HOC.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Commencement of Production" to "Operation Start" and the corresponding redefinition creates a direct conflict. The original text defines 'Commencement of Production' as 'production and delivery to the point of sale (refiner)', while the changed text defines 'Operation Start' as 'extraction and transport to the processing plant'. These definitions refer to different stages of the production process, leading to ambiguity and inconsistency in determining when HOC's exclusive financing rights end. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty about the obligations of both parties, making enforcement unclear. The contradiction arises because the section specifies exclusive financing rights until one event, but the redefinition makes it unclear which event terminates the right.

perturbation.original_text: If, but only if HOC exercises the Option and completes the acquisition of the Additional Shares, HOC shall be entitled to nominate one (1) individual to the Board of Directors (the "HOC Director"). Upon receipt of such nomination and its satisfaction that the individual nominated by HOC meets the qualification requirements for directors under applicable laws, the Board of Directors further agrees to expand its membership to four (4) positions and appoint the individual nominated by HOC to the newly-created vacancy. The Board of Directors further agrees that if HOC maintains a Pro Rata Interest of at least 14.5%, it shall nominate such individual to the slate of directors at each subsequent annual meeting so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked.
perturbation.changed_text: If, but only if HOC exercises the Option and completes the acquisition of the Additional Shares, HOC shall be entitled to nominate one (1) individual to the Board of Directors (the "HOC Representative"). Upon receipt of such nomination and its satisfaction that the individual nominated by HOC meets the qualification requirements for directors under applicable laws, the Board of Directors further agrees to expand its membership to four (4) positions and appoint the individual nominated by HOC to the newly-created vacancy. The Board of Directors further agrees that if HOC maintains a Pro Rata Interest of at least 14.5%, it shall nominate such individual to the slate of directors at each subsequent annual meeting so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "HOC Director" to "HOC Representative" introduces an in-text contradiction because the term 'Director' has specific legal implications related to fiduciary duties and liabilities, while 'Representative' does not. Later in Section 5.4 and 5.5, the agreement refers back to 'HOC Director' when discussing resignation and indemnification. This misalignment creates confusion about whether the nominated individual is subject to the same responsibilities and protections as other directors or if their role is merely representative, creating uncertainty and possibly conflicting obligations, especially given they will now be labeled as HOC Representative.
perturbation.contradicted_text: So long as HOC is entitled to nominate and maintain a director pursuant to this Article 5, the Company shall indemnify each current and former HOC Director and shall maintain director's and officer's liability insurance for the benefit of each such director, with the same rights and benefits as are accorded the directors of the Company generally.
perturbation.justification: The change from "HOC Director" to "HOC Representative" creates a contradiction because the contradicted text specifically refers to the indemnification and insurance coverage for "HOC Director." By changing the title, the agreement becomes ambiguous as to whether the nominated individual, now an "HOC Representative," is entitled to the same rights and protections as other directors. This misalignment leads to uncertainty and a potential conflict, as the Company is only obligated to indemnify and insure "HOC Directors," and the nominated individual is no longer explicitly referred to as such. This is a misaligned terminology in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: HOC covenants and agrees that, except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, HOC will not, and will not allow any HOC Entities to, in either case, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (i) acquire or enter into any agreement to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire in any manner any equity securities of the Company (whether issued or unissued) that would result in a Pro Rata Interest greater than 40% other than (A) as a result of a stock dividend or distribution made by or a recapitalization of the Company, (B) in accordance with the terms of any dividend reinvestment or share purchase plan made available from time to time by the Company to holders of equity securities; (C) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to a rights offering made by the Company to the holders of its equity securities; or (D) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to any shareholder rights plan of the Company and attached to equity securities;
perturbation.changed_text: HOC covenants and agrees that, except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, HOC will not, and will not allow any HOC Entities to, in either case, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (i) acquire or enter into any agreement to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire in any manner any corporate holdings of the Company (whether issued or unissued) that would result in a Pro Rata Interest greater than 40% other than (A) as a result of a stock dividend or distribution made by or a recapitalization of the Company, (B) in accordance with the terms of any dividend reinvestment or share purchase plan made available from time to time by the Company to holders of equity securities; (C) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to a rights offering made by the Company to the holders of its equity securities; or (D) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to any shareholder rights plan of the Company and attached to equity securities;
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'equity securities' with 'corporate holdings,' the scope of the standstill provision becomes unclear. 'Equity securities' has a defined legal meaning, whereas 'corporate holdings' is vague and could be interpreted to include assets other than securities, such as real estate or intellectual property. This leads to an in-text contradiction because the exceptions listed in (A) through (D) still refer to 'equity securities,' creating ambiguity as to whether these exceptions apply to the newly defined 'corporate holdings' or just the traditional understanding of equity securities. This contradiction creates significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the standstill agreement and the types of acquisitions restricted by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HOC covenants and agrees that, except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, HOC will not, and will not allow any HOC Entities to, in either case, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (i) acquire or enter into any agreement to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire in any manner any corporate holdings of the Company (whether issued or unissued) that would result in a Pro Rata Interest greater than 40% other than (A) as a result of a stock dividend or distribution made by or a recapitalization of the Company, (B) in accordance with the terms of any dividend reinvestment or share purchase plan made available from time to time by the Company to holders of equity securities; (C) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to a rights offering made by the Company to the holders of its equity securities; or (D) pursuant to the exercise of rights issued pursuant to any shareholder rights plan of the Company and attached to equity securities;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'equity securities' to 'corporate holdings' introduces a strong contradiction. While the original text restricts acquisition of 'equity securities' exceeding a certain threshold, the changed text broadens this to 'corporate holdings,' which is undefined. The listed exceptions (A) through (D) explicitly refer to 'equity securities.' Therefore, it is unclear whether these exceptions apply to the broader 'corporate holdings,' causing a direct conflict in the text. This contradiction makes the standstill provision ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The changed text's scope is different from the contradicted text even though they are identical.
